labs.clinicalContext: Monitor kidney function as empagliflozin causes an initial reversible decline in eGFR. Efficacy requires eGFR ≥20 ml/min per 1.73 m² for heart failure indication, ≥30 ml/min per 1.73 m² for diabetes indication

labs.actionRequired: Evaluate for kidney function deterioration if doubling occurs
labs.clinicalContext: Monitor for doubling of serum creatinine which was a component of kidney outcomes in EMPA-REG trial
Monitor albuminuria progression as empagliflozin reduces progression to severely increased albuminuria (ACR >300 mg/g)

labRequirements.specialPopulationNotes: eGFR ≥30 ml/min per 1.73 m² required for diabetes indication; eGFR ≥20 ml/min per 1.73 m² for heart failure indication. Monitor for initial eGFR decline which is typically reversible.
labRequirements.sourceSnippet: EMPA-REG enrolled patients with eGFR ≥30 ml/min per 1.73 m². EMPEROR trials enrolled patients with eGFR ≥20. Post hoc analyses suggested that a drop in eGFR ≥10% was not associated with increased risk. Incident or worsening nephropathy defined as progression to severely increased albuminuria (ACR >300 mg/g), doubling of serum creatinine, accompanied by eGFR ≤45 ml/min per 1.73 m², or initiation of kidney replacement therapy.
